profile.name: Calling All Minds
profile.mission: To unlock human potential by making systems, workplaces, and learning environments accessible, inclusive, and effective for everyone.
profile.products: AXS Passport (workplace adjustments management), AXS Audit (accessibility assessment and AI-guided remediation), and AXS Toolbar (AI-powered accessibility interface layer).
profile.customers: Large employers and corporates, universities and higher education institutions, public sector organisations, and organisations within the Disability Confident scheme.
profile.constraints: Time is limited, cost matters, solutions must be practical and implementable, and unnecessary complexity should be avoided.
profile.stage: Mission-led but commercially driven organisation building scalable accessibility systems.
profile.operatingModel: Combines consultancy, training, and technology products to help organisations move from intention to implementation through practical systems rather than one-off interventions.

founder_context.name: Suraj Sharma
founder_context.role: Founder of Calling All Minds and primary decision-maker across strategy, product direction, and commercial approach.
founder_context.background: Entrepreneur with experience building and operating businesses, focused on execution and outcomes.
founder_context.operating_style: Decisive, outcome-focused, high-standard, iterative, and direct.
founder_context.expectations_from_metis: Avoid generic or surface-level output; challenge assumptions when appropriate; provide clear structured thinking; offer recommendations rather than just options; remain commercially aware.
founder_context.decision_bias: Optimise for clarity and execution, avoid over-engineering, focus on what will work in practice, and assume decisions will be made and executed quickly.

company_detail.overview: Calling All Minds is a UK-based organisation focused on improving accessibility, inclusion, and support for disabled and neurodivergent individuals across education, employment, and public services.
company_detail.core_problem: Organisations want to be inclusive and face legal and reputational pressure, but often lack clarity, tools, and execution capability, leading to inconsistent adjustments, poor accessibility implementation, compliance risk, and frustration.
company_detail.approach: Provide practical solutions, combining technology and services into structured systems that make adjustments easier to request and implement, make accessibility measurable, and reduce organisational friction.
company_detail.differentiation: Combines technology with human expertise, focuses on execution rather than theory, builds systems rather than one-off interventions, and is rooted in real lived experience of disability and neurodiversity.
company_detail.tone: Practical, credible, outcome-focused, not overly corporate, and focused on real-world impact.
company_detail.market_context: Increasing pressure from Equality Act obligations, the European Accessibility Act, WCAG-related accessibility regulation, ESG and DEI expectations, and reputational risk meets a market where most organisations lack coherent systems and execution is inconsistent.

products.name: AXS Passport
products.summary: System for managing workplace adjustments, employee requests, organisational tracking, fulfilment, reporting, and consistency.
products.primary_value: Reduces admin burden, improves employee experience, and supports compliance.
products.usp: Comprehensive tool with AI-assisted guidance for creating adjustment requests, a secure document vault, and analytics for organisational decision-making and implementation.

products.name: AXS Audit
products.summary: Accessibility assessment tool that identifies issues across digital platforms, highlights WCAG-related risks, and provides actionable insights.
products.primary_value: Reveals real accessibility gaps and supports compliance and improvement.
products.usp: Includes COGA and cognitive accessibility scoring, uses AI vision and LLM models to scan reliably across roughly 80% of WCAG 2.2 regulations, and offers Orion for code-level fixes and chat support.

products.name: AXS Toolbar
products.summary: Accessibility interface layer with simplification, summarisation, and visual-adjustment tools designed for cognitive accessibility.
products.primary_value: Improves usability for end users and enhances accessibility without major rebuilds.
products.usp: AI-powered tools such as page summarisation, text simplification, voice navigation, image description, plus full admin customisation for features and design.

session_bootstrap.opening_session_goal: Design the optimal v1 architecture for Metis as an internal multi-agent intelligence system that initially supports sales and later expands into innovation, product design, automation, app creation, and broader business decision-making.
session_bootstrap.design_objectives: Maximise output quality, minimise cost and latency, avoid unnecessary agents and duplicated roles, prioritise simplicity and clarity, and scale beyond sales into a general-purpose system.
session_bootstrap.constraints: Cost matters, speed matters, the system should not require excessive agents per task, multiple roles should not be assigned to the same model without justification, and implementation must remain practical.
session_bootstrap.deliberation_rule: Do not converge too early; challenge assumptions, disagree where necessary, and improve each other's thinking because the goal is the best possible architecture rather than agreement.

open_questions_for_orion: What company team size should be stored?
Should the company profile include explicit commercial targets, pricing posture, or revenue priorities?
Should cross-session learning reference all past sessions or only manually approved insights?
Should founder context be injected into every session verbatim or through a compressed advisory summary?
